fields.summary: Queue Mapping could provide options to provide 'user' specific auto-created queues under a specified group parent queue
[Umbrella] Capacity Scheduler: Support Auto Creation of Leaf Queues While Doing Queue Mapping

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.
status.name: Open

issuetype.description: A new feature of the product, which has yet to be developed.
issuetype.name: New Feature

fields.description:
Current Queue-Mapping only provides %user as an option for 'user' specific queues as u:%user:%user. We can also support %user with group as 'g:makerting-group:marketing.%user'  and user specific queues can be automatically created under a group queue in this case.

cc [~leftnoteasy]

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

Patch looks good to me. Just one question, is there a reason we remove the timeout for tests modified in TestCapacitySchedulerAutoQueueCreation?

Thanks [~Zian Chen] for reviewing the patch. Reverted changes for timeout removal. 

[~wangda] Can you pls review and commit if it looks okay?

[~leftnoteasy] Thanks for the review. Currently we do not support for g:makerting-group:%user. Supporting this case seems valid like you mentioned - queue mapping for user from specific groups alone instead of just u:%user:%user. Have added this in the latest patch and also added end to end tests with normal queue mapping along with the auto creation of queue behaviour tests with parent queue.

[~leftnoteasy] Thanks for the review. Currently we do not support for g:marketing-group:%user. Supporting this case seems valid like you mentioned - queue mapping for user from specific groups alone instead of just u:%user:%user. Have added this in the latest patch and also added end to end tests with normal queue mapping along with the auto creation of queue behaviour tests with parent queue.
Thanks [~suma.shivaprasad], the latest patch looks good, plz confirm the UT failure is not related. Will commit tomorrow if no objections.

[~leftnoteasy] Thanks!   The UT failure in unrelated and related to ResourceTracker

Failures: [ERROR] TestResourceTrackerService.testNodeRemovalGracefully:1608->testNodeRemovalUtilLost:1877 There should be no Lost NMs! expected:<2> but was:<0> [INFO]
Committed to trunk, thanks [~suma.shivaprasad] and thanks reviews from [~Zian Chen]

SUCCESS: Integrated in Jenkins build Hadoop-trunk-Commit #13833 (See [https://builds.apache.org/job/Hadoop-trunk-Commit/13833/])
YARN-7657. Queue Mapping could provide options to provide 'user' (wangda: rev b167d60763f4b9ac69976df2da5d988e5faa85e0)

[~leftnoteasy] Thanks for the review. Currently we do not support for g:marketing-group:%user. Supporting this case seems valid like you mentioned - queue mapping for user from specific groups alone instead of just u:%user:%user. Have added this in the latest patch and also added end to end tests with normal queue mapping along with the auto creation of queue behaviour tests with parent queue.
Thanks [~suma.shivaprasad], the latest patch looks good, plz confirm the UT failure is not related. Will commit tomorrow if no objections.

[~leftnoteasy] Thanks!   The UT failure in unrelated and related to ResourceTracker
